skill.name: ultrathink-trigger
skill.description: Auto-escalate reasoning depth for complex coding, architecture, debugging, and unfamiliar code problems.
skill.summary: Trigger deeper analysis when a task crosses defined complexity signals.
skill.author: DMJGilbert

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution and ordinary text as weak cryptography. Review of SKILL.md found no executable scripts, network access, filesystem access, credential handling, or prompt-injection language. The remaining risk is low because the skill changes prompting behavior and may increase analysis time.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are markdown fenced code examples and inline flag names. They do not invoke Ruby, shell commands, or a runtime execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation examples in SKILL.md. No command runner, shell interpreter, script file, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is Textual False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography hits occur in descriptive text and an example prompt. No hashing, encryption, crypto library, or algorithm implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter description text, and line 101 is a sample prompt phrase. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic use.

content.user_title: Trigger Deep Analysis for Complex Tasks
content.value_statement: Complex coding problems often fail when the model uses shallow analysis too long. This skill defines signals and prompts that trigger deeper reasoning for architecture, debugging, security, and unfamiliar code.
actual_capabilities: Defines complexity indicators for multi-file work, architecture decisions, security, performance, and breaking changes.
Sets a threshold that recommends deeper analysis when complexity weight reaches five or more.
Provides trigger phrases for extended analysis on complex prompts.
Describes integration points for fix, brainstorm, orchestrator, and sequential-thinking workflows.
Lists situations where deeper analysis should be used proactively.
Identifies anti-patterns such as using deep analysis for simple tasks.
limitations: It does not execute code, inspect repositories, or validate solutions by itself.
It depends on the host AI tool honoring deeper reasoning prompts.
It can increase response time and token use on complex tasks.
It provides guidance only and does not replace testing or human review.

use_cases.title: Architecture Decision Review
use_cases.description: Use the complexity checklist before choosing patterns, data flow, or module boundaries for a significant change.

use_cases.title: Debugging After Failed Fixes
use_cases.description: Trigger deeper analysis after repeated failed attempts to revisit assumptions, root causes, and missing checks.

use_cases.title: Security-Sensitive Planning
use_cases.description: Escalate reasoning when a task touches authentication, permissions, secrets, or other high-impact security areas.

prompt_templates.title: Check Task Complexity
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for deciding whether deeper analysis is needed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this task against the complexity indicators. Tell me whether it should trigger deeper analysis and why.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Failed Fixes
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for repeated debugging failures.
prompt_templates.prompt: ultrathink: What are all likely root causes for this issue? List my assumptions, missing evidence, and the next checks to run.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Architecture Options
prompt_templates.prompt: ultrathink: Compare these architecture options for maintainability, performance, security, migration risk, and long-term complexity.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a High-Risk Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for multi-domain or breaking changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: ultrathink: Build an implementation plan for this change. Include dependencies, failure modes, test strategy, rollout steps, and rollback criteria.

output_examples.input: A developer is changing authentication across frontend, backend, and database code.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends deeper analysis because the task crosses multiple domains and has security implications. The response should examine assumptions, permission boundaries, migration risk, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: A simple typo fix affects one known file.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends staying in normal mode because the task is simple, localized, and well understood.

output_examples.input: Two fixes have failed and the root cause remains unclear.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a deeper debugging prompt focused on root causes, untested assumptions, missing logs, and alternative explanations.

best_practices: Score complexity before major architecture, security, or migration work.
Use deeper analysis after repeated failed fixes instead of repeating the same approach.
Pair deeper reasoning with concrete verification steps and tests.
anti_patterns: Using deep analysis for simple, local, well-understood edits.
Skipping deeper analysis when the task affects security, data integrity, or public APIs.
Treating a longer analysis as proof without running checks or reviewing evidence.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands or modify files?
faq.answer: No. It provides prompting guidance for when to use deeper analysis.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it for architecture decisions, repeated debugging failures, security-sensitive work, performance hotspots, breaking changes, or unfamiliar code.

faq.question: Does it work with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Codex, Claude, and Claude Code as prompting guidance.

faq.question: Will it make every response better?
faq.answer: No. It is best for complex tasks and can be inefficient for simple work.

faq.question: Can it replace tests?
faq.answer: No. It helps plan and analyze work, but verification still requires tests, logs, or review.

faq.question: Why was the security risk marked low?
faq.answer: Static alerts were false positives from markdown text. The low rating reflects minimal residual risk from prompting behavior and longer analysis.
